stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and transformer architectures.
The associated tags indicate relevance to text generation, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, llama, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern AI/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Massive breach spills credentials for thousands of sensitive networks
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A massive security breach has exposed credentials across numerous sensitive networks.
High-profile organizations affected include Oracle, Lenovo, FedEx, and Fortinet.
The breach impacts critical infrastructure, including a NATO contractor.

stories.title: Noam Shazeer Joins OpenAI
stories.author: lukasgross
bullet_points: Noam Shazeer, a co-lead on Google's Gemini, is joining OpenAI.
This move signifies a significant talent shift between two major players in the AI industry.
The news is highly relevant as it impacts the competitive landscape of large language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in its MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI capabilities on Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating ongoing development and performance improvements.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing.
The core value proposition is running AI tools privately and locally on the user's own device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Sooner than expected? Useful quantum error correction promised for 2028.
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Quantum error correction, a critical hurdle for practical quantum computing, is projected to be useful by 2028.
The article suggests progress is happening sooner than previously anticipated in the field.
The discussion frames the advancement within the broader context of quantum hardware development alongside classical computing advancements.

stories.title: Data Intelligence Agents: Interpreting, Modeling, and Querying Enterprise Data via Autonomous Coding Agents
stories.author: Henrik Ohlsson
bullet_points: Introduces Data Intelligence Agents (DIA) to automate enterprise data integration, addressing bottlenecks in manual data handoffs.
The system utilizes autonomous coding agents (ACAs) to handle data discovery, structuring, and querying autonomously.
DIA comprises three specialized agents: Data Interpreter, Schema Creator, and Query Generator, which generate, execute, and repair code.

stories.title: Diffusion-Proof: Recipe for Formal Theorem Proving Beyond Auto-Regressive Generation
stories.author: Tong Zhang
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in using standard Auto-Regressive (AR) LLMs for formal theorem proving.
It proposes a new approach, 'Diffusion-Proof,' to enhance the formal mathematical reasoning of LLMs.
The work aims to overcome issues like long-range coherence and compounding errors inherent in next-token prediction methods.

stories.title: I found 10k GitHub repositories distributing Trojan malware
stories.author: theorchid
bullet_points: A significant security vulnerability was discovered involving Trojan malware.
The malware was found distributed across a large volume (10k) of GitHub repositories.
This highlights a major risk vector within open-source software development.

stories.title: Agentic Resource Discovery Specification
stories.author: damick
bullet_points: Google has announced the Agentic Resource Discovery Specification.
This specification relates to enabling agents to discover and interact with resources.
It signals a move toward more sophisticated, autonomous agent capabilities in software.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Snap’s Specs look good on nobody
stories.author: David Pierce
bullet_points: Snap has released new smart glasses featuring advanced face-computer technology.
The glasses are noted for their compact design, avoiding the bulk of traditional VR headsets.
The high price point of $2,195 is mentioned as a potential barrier to adoption.

stories.title: Amazon employees say they’re facing termination for backing data center limits
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Amazon employees allege retaliation after testifying before Seattle City Council regarding data center limits.
The engineers claim their employer violated anti-discrimination laws related to political speech.
The dispute highlights growing tension between major tech companies and local regulatory/political oversight.

stories.title: Before SpaceX IPO, investors in China secretly acquired stakes
stories.author: stin Elliott and Joshua Kaplan, ProPublica
bullet_points: A previously undisclosed investor in SpaceX has been linked to Chinese military contractors.
The story raises geopolitical and national security concerns regarding foreign investment in US aerospace technology.
The revelation surfaces ahead of SpaceX's potential Initial Public Offering (IPO).

stories.title: Bernie Sanders unveils $7 trillion plan to give Americans control of AI industry
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Bernie Sanders proposed a massive $7 trillion plan aimed at giving Americans greater control over the AI industry.
The proposal is expected to cause significant pushback from major AI corporations.
The core of the plan involves establishing an AI wealth fund to democratize control over AI technology.

stories.title: California says AT&T lied to FCC in attempt to shut off old phone network
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: California is challenging AT&T's actions regarding the discontinuation of an older phone network.
The core issue involves AT&T's petitions to the FCC to override state regulations.
The FCC is currently reviewing these petitions concerning the service shutdown.

stories.title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Nicholas Rhinehart
bullet_points: Introduces UBP2, a model-based approach for Preference-based Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Addresses the sample inefficiency of existing preference-based RL methods by actively directing exploration.
Jointly reasons over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions to improve learning.

stories.title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
stories.author: Rex Ying
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in post-training language models, specifically the high cost and potential noise in Chain-of-Thought (CoT) annotations used for supervised distillation.
It proposes 'Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation' as an alternative to traditional supervised distillation and RLHF.
The research aims to improve model learning by moving beyond relying solely on perfect, expensive human-provided rationales or compressed reward signals.

stories.title: Reference-Driven Multi-Speaker Audio Scene Generation from In-the-Wild Priors
stories.author: Yoav HaCohen
bullet_points: Introduces ScenA, a new method for multi-speaker audio scene generation.
Differs from traditional systems by conditioning a text-to-audio flow-matching model directly on multiple reference voices.
Aims to generate realistic conversational audio that includes ambient, 'in-the-wild' background textures, moving beyond clean speech-only pipelines.

stories.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
stories.author: Jacob Andreas
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for interpretable deep learning by aiming to replace opaque neural computations with symbolic descriptions.
The paper proposes approximating the behavior of deep network components, specifically attention heads in transformer models, using executable programs.
The methodology involves computing attention matrices on training examples and then using a pre-trained language model for program synthesis.

stories.title: Correct Yourself, Keep My Trust: How Self-Correction and Social Connection Shape Credibility in Social Chatbots
stories.author: Yi-Chieh Lee
bullet_points: Investigates how social chatbots recover from errors to maintain user trust.
Compares three distinct error correction strategies (webpage retraction vs. self-correction).
Highlights the critical role of social connection in amplifying the impact of chatbot inaccuracies.

stories.title: NeSyCat Torch: A Differentiable Tensor Implementation of Categorical Semantics for Neurosymbolic Learning
stories.author: Björn Gehrke
bullet_points: Introduces NeSyCat Torch, a differentiable tensor implementation designed to unify fragmented approaches in neurosymbolic learning.
The core concept aims to subsume various truth definitions (classical, fuzzy, probabilistic, neural) under a single inductive definition.
The work bridges the gap by allowing computational symbols, typically handled by neural networks, to be interpreted within the NeSyCat framework.

stories.title: A Multi-Domain Benchmark for Detecting AI-Generated Text-Rich Images from GPT-Image-2
stories.author: Yuqi Ouyang
bullet_points: The research introduces a new multi-domain benchmark specifically designed to detect AI-generated text-rich images.
The challenge addresses the growing risk posed by multimodal models synthesizing realistic, potentially sensitive textual content in images.
This work aims to improve digital trust and content authenticity by moving beyond traditional object-centric image benchmarks.

stories.title: X+Slides: Benchmarking Audience-Conditioned Slide Generation
stories.author: Fan Wu
bullet_points: Introduces X+Slides, a new benchmark for evaluating LLM-based slide generation.
Addresses a gap in current benchmarks by focusing on audience conditioning, not just technical completeness.
Highlights the real-world need to tailor presentations (e.g., proofs for specialists vs. conclusions for decision-makers).

stories.title: Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States
stories.author: Diag Davenport
bullet_points: LOCUS is a new corpus designed to provide machine-readable access to US local ordinances.
Local ordinances are currently fragmented and inaccessible for large-scale AI/ML legal research.
The corpus covers critical areas like zoning, housing, and public health regulation.

stories.title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Nicholas Rhinehart
bullet_points: Introduces UBP2, a model-based approach for Preference-based Reinforcement Learning (RL).
Addresses the sample inefficiency of existing methods by actively directing exploration.
Jointly reasons over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions.

stories.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
stories.author: Jacob Andreas
bullet_points: The research addresses the interpretability challenge in deep learning by aiming to replace opaque neural computations with human-readable symbolic descriptions.
The paper proposes using program synthesis to approximate the behavior of specific components, focusing on attention heads within transformer language models.
The methodology involves computing attention matrices on training examples and then using a pre-trained language model to generate executable programs that mimic the head's function.

stories.title: Confidence is Not Reliability: Rethinking MC Dropout in Brain Tumour Segmentation
stories.author: Nishant Ravikumar
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical safety risk of segmentation models failing silently in brain tumor (glioma) analysis.
It investigates if Monte Carlo (MC) Dropout can reliably estimate voxel-level uncertainty to detect segmentation errors in clinically vital regions.
The study questions the sufficiency of standard metrics (like Dice scores) for ensuring model reliability in high-stakes medical applications.

stories.title: Does VLA Even Know the Basics? Measuring Commonsense and World Knowledge Retention in Vision-Language-Action Models
stories.author: Vlad Shakhuro
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical gap in Embodied Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: quantifying retained commonsense and world knowledge after fine-tuning on robotics data.
Existing evaluation methods are ambiguous, often confusing a lack of factual knowledge with poor low-level control generalization.
The authors introduce 'Act2Answer,' a new protocol that adapts standard VLM knowledge benchmarks to VLA evaluation by forcing agents to answer through physical action.

stories.title: Risk Stratification for ICU Delirium using Pervasive Ambient Sensing Information
stories.author: Parisa Rashidi
bullet_points: The study addresses ICU delirium, a serious complication linked to increased morbidity and healthcare costs.
It investigates the potential of pervasive ambient sensing data (light intensity and sound pressure levels) for early risk stratification.
This research suggests environmental factors, often overlooked, can be used to predict the onset of delirium.

stories.title: Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: Introduces OmniAgent, a novel native omni-modal agent for video understanding.
Addresses the computational inefficiency of passive video models by moving beyond uniform frame processing.
Frames video understanding as a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle to manage context cost.

stories.title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
stories.author: Yoon Kim
bullet_points: Introduces Turing-RL, a new reinforcement learning approach for training user simulators.
Addresses a limitation in existing methods that rely on matching a single ground truth response.
Potential applications span agent assistant training, personalization system evaluation, and social science research.

stories.title: Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States
stories.author: Diag Davenport
bullet_points: LOCUS is a new corpus designed to provide machine-readable access to local ordinances across the United States.
Local ordinances are a critical, yet largely absent, data source for legal AI applications.
The project addresses the fragmentation of local codes, which are currently optimized for human browsing rather than bulk research.

stories.title: Enhancing Decision-Making with Large Language Models through Multi-Agent Fictitious Play
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation of current LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) in complex decision-making tasks.
It proposes using 'Multi-Agent Fictitious Play' to enable simultaneous, interdependent reasoning from multiple stakeholders.
This work aims to improve LLM decision-making where tasks cannot be solved by simple subtask distribution.

stories.title: Structured Inference with Large Language Gibbs
stories.author: Esmeralda S. Whitammer
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of performing probabilistically coherent structured reasoning using Large Language Models (LLMs).
Introduces 'Large Language Gibbs,' a new scheme for structured probabilistic inference.
Uses the conditional distributions of an LLM to function as transition operators in an iterative process, moving beyond single-pass autoregressive generation.

stories.title: DreamReasoner-8B: Block-Size Curriculum Learning for Diffusion Reasoning Models
stories.author: Lingpeng Kong
bullet_points: Introduces DreamReasoner-8B, an open-source block diffusion reasoning model designed to address scaling issues in block diffusion language models.
The research systematically investigates how training and inference block sizes impact long Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
Findings indicate a significant performance disparity, showing that training with large block sizes leads to poor reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: RECOM: A Validity Discrimination Tradeoff in Automatic Metrics for Open Ended Reddit Question Answering
stories.author: Tathagata Mukherjee
bullet_points: The paper addresses a fundamental tension in LLM evaluation: balancing content validity (alignment) against discriminative power (ranking models).
RECOM introduces a new, contamination-free dataset of 15,000 r/AskReddit questions specifically designed for open-ended QA evaluation.
This work highlights the challenges of using automatic metrics for subjective, opinion-driven tasks like Reddit question answering.

stories.title: Launch HN: TesterArmy (YC P26) – Agents that test web and mobile apps
stories.author: okwasniewski
bullet_points: TesterArmy is an agentic testing platform designed for web and mobile applications.
It allows users to define end-to-end tests using natural language instead of manual effort or static scripts.
The platform utilizes AI agents to execute tests both before deployment and in production.

stories.title: Cosmic as Agent Memory: Structured, Versioned, and Queryable
stories.author: tonyspiro
bullet_points: The article discusses using 'Cosmic' as a mechanism for AI agent memory.
This memory system is highlighted as being structured, versioned, and queryable.
The underlying premise is that AI agents improve with cumulative experience (conversations, tasks, prompts).
